title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Hercule Poirot reveals that a flaw in the killer's plan has allowed him to identify the murderer of Ingrid Brent. Using a poem about a missing horseshoe nail to illustrate how small details cause large failures, Poirot explains that Ingrid Brent's broken shoe heel led to her discovery of a lie. On September 9th, Ingrid's heel became stuck in a grating, causing her to return to the Cavenish Typewriting Agency earlier than expected. While there, she realized that Miss Martindeer’s testimony regarding a specific phone call at 1:49 p.m. was false, as no such call occurred.

Poirot deduces that Miss Martindeer followed Ingrid to Wilbraham Crescent to silence her. While Ingrid was in a phone booth, Miss Martindeer strangled her with a scarf. Poirot identifies Miss Marple as the killer, describing her as an efficient and unimaginative conspirator. 

The motive stems from a fraudulent inheritance scheme involving the Brand couple. Poirot explains that the Brands used a previous marriage to claim a large inheritance from a Canadian uncle, despite the current Mrs. Brand not being the rightful heir. To prevent the truth from being exposed by a visitor from Canada, the Brands conspired to murder him. Poirot concludes that Miss Martin and Mrs. Brand are sisters and the masterminds behind the plot. He further suggests that the killer attempted to evade suspicion by posing as a wealthy tourist and disposing of the victim's passport to misdirect the investigation.
